claim: Token maxing (token burning for 8-9 hours of agent looping) at scale is being replaced by opinionated bot looping with tighter reward mechanisms and agent management
actionable: Move from unguided token maxing to structured agent swimlanes with reward signals and active monitoring; this is 2026 efficiency
source_title: Dark Factory: OpenClaw Ships Faster Than You Can Read the Diff

claim: Reading reasoning tokens (inferring agent quality from how it explains itself, not just outputs) is a key skill for managing autonomous agent swarms
actionable: Develop intuition for detecting agent 'bullshitting' by analyzing reasoning token patterns; nuke sessions that aren't making coherent sense
source_title: Dark Factory: OpenClaw Ships Faster Than You Can Read the Diff

claim: Swim lanes (15-20 parallel coding sessions organized by type: CI, features, bugs, refactoring) allow soft skill management of agents like managing human staff
actionable: Organize parallel agent work into swim lanes by task type; apply human management skills (delegation, monitoring, decision-making) at scale
source_title: Dark Factory: OpenClaw Ships Faster Than You Can Read the Diff

claim: 2025 was about token maxing; 2026 is about token efficiency and not wasting them through smart agent orchestration and swimlane management
actionable: Focus on token efficiency, agent in-the-loop decision points, and selective high-value work rather than burning tokens indiscriminately
source_title: Dark Factory: OpenClaw Ships Faster Than You Can Read the Diff

claim: Large-scale code refactoring (2,700 commits, 1M lines changed, 82% codebase touched in one night) is achievable with opinionated agent coordination
actionable: Use coordinated agent swarms for large structural changes; modern agents can handle massive refactors with proper orchestration
source_title: Dark Factory: OpenClaw Ships Faster Than You Can Read the Diff
